tasks.title: Write explanation of DFS
tasks.description: Write a clear explanation of Depth-First Search in the section for Question 1.

tasks.title: Write explanation of backtracking
tasks.description: Write a description of backtracking in the same section for Question 1.

tasks.title: Compare DFS and BFS
tasks.description: Write a comparison of DFS and Breadth-First Search, highlighting the differences in the section for Question 1.

tasks.title: Trace DFS on given graph
tasks.description: Trace the traversal order and show recursive call flow for the provided graph (A — B — C, A — D, B — E) in the section for Question 2.

tasks.title: Implement recursive DFS
tasks.description: Implement the recursive DFS function in the file dfs.py.

tasks.title: Implement iterative DFS
tasks.description: Implement the iterative DFS function using a stack in the file dfs.py.

tasks.title: Write explanation using maze example
tasks.description: Explain backtracking by using a maze example in the section for Question 5.

tasks.title: Write explanation using tree traversal
tasks.description: Explain backtracking using a tree traversal example in the same section for Question 5.

tasks.title: Explain cycle detection in DFS
tasks.description: Write an explanation of how DFS can detect cycles in graphs in the section for Question 6.

tasks.title: Explain DFS complexity O(V + E)
tasks.description: Write an explanation of why DFS has a time complexity of O(V + E) in the section for Question 7.

tasks.title: Explain finding connected components
tasks.description: Describe how DFS finds the number of connected components in the section for Question 8.

tasks.title: Explain DFS-based topological sorting
tasks.description: Write an explanation of how DFS can be used for topological sorting in the section for Question 9.

tasks.title: Define edge types in DFS
tasks.description: Define and explain tree edge, back edge, forward edge, and cross edge in the section for Question 10.

tasks.title: Final review and submission
tasks.description: Review the entire document, package it, and submit the assignment.
